heading: Application of treaties; exceptions
label: 1489 Application of treaties; exceptions
text: Nothing in this subchapter shall be applied in contravention of the provisions of any treaty or convention to which the United States is a party and which has been ratified by the Senate before : , That no woman who was a national of the United States shall be deemed to have lost her nationality solely by reason of her marriage to an alien on or after , or to an alien racially ineligible to citizenship on or after , or, in the case of a woman who was a United States citizen at birth, through residence abroad following such marriage, notwithstanding the provisions of any existing treaty or convention.December 25, 1952September 22, 1922March 3, 1931Provided, however
notes: Amendments1988— substituted “before ” for “upon the effective date of this subchapter”.Pub. L. 100–525December 25, 1952
amendments: Amendments1988— substituted “before ” for “upon the effective date of this subchapter”.Pub. L. 100–525December 25, 1952
